question: Which of the vector operations shown below is NOT $O(1)$?

answers.feedback: Correct! Erasing the first element requires all subsequent elements to be shifted one position to the left, taking $O(n)$ time.
No. pop_back() removes the last element, which is an $O(1)$ operation because no shifting is required.
No. push_back() adds an element at the end of the vector, which is $O(1)$ (amortized).
No. Accessing an element by index is a direct memory lookup, which is $O(1)$.
